name: Sweet Pongal Recipe | Sakkarai Pongal | Chakkara Pongal

ingredients.name: moong dal
ingredients.notes: 85 grams, husked and split mung lentils

ingredients.name: jaggery
ingredients.notes: 115 to 120 grams, chopped and tightly packed

ingredients.name: green cardamoms
ingredients.notes: or ½ teaspoon green cardamom powder

steps.instruction: Pick rice and moong dal to remove stones. Roast both together on low heat till aromatic.
Rinse roasted rice and moong dal a couple of times. Drain well.
Add to pressure cooker with 3 cups water. Pressure cook for 8 to 9 whistles or 11 to 12 minutes on medium heat.
Let pressure settle naturally. Open lid, check consistency. If separate like pulao, add ½ to 1 cup hot water. Mash slightly.
Crush seeds of 5 green cardamoms + 1 clove (or use ½ teaspoon cardamom powder).
In a pan, dissolve chopped jaggery in ½ cup water on low to medium heat.
Strain the syrup if needed and add to the cooked rice-lentil mixture.
Add crushed cardamom powder and a pinch of edible camphor. Mix well.
Heat 4 to 5 tablespoons ghee. Fry cashews till light golden, then add raisins and fry till plump.
Add the fried cashews, raisins and ghee to the pongal. Mix thoroughly.

tips: Adjust water and whistles depending on quality of moong dal.
Can be made on stove-top with 4 to 4.5 cups water.
Pongal thickens as it cools. Add hot water or milk to adjust consistency.
Store leftovers in fridge for 1-2 days.
variations: Add milk instead of some water for richer taste.
Replace jaggery with sugar, coconut sugar or palm sugar.
Make with cracked wheat (dalia), millets or rolled oats.
